result.summary: custhelp.com (Oracle B2C Service / RightNow) exposes tenant-scoped SOAP and REST integration surfaces, including Connect Web Services for SOAP, Connect REST API, Chat SOAP/UQ APIs, and Element Manager public REST APIs; all require authentication.
result.description: custhelp.com hosts Oracle B2C Service (formerly RightNow) customer support and service software resources, including support portals and technical documentation for tenant sites. The platform supports customer service workflows, knowledge, chat, and agent-facing extensibility and integration capabilities.

agent_basic.label: Oracle B2C Service agent account username/password
agent_basic.setup: Create or use an Oracle B2C Service staff account and enable the needed API permissions on its profile. For SOAP, enable `Public SOAP API` under Configuration > Staff Management > Profiles > Permissions > Administration as described in [Connect Web Services for SOAP access](https://cx.rightnow.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/4832). Oracle recommends creating a dedicated account for API use. REST session auth can also use an agent account to mint a session token via `AgentAuthenticator()` as shown in [Session authentication for REST API](https://cx.rightnow.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/11153).

rest_session_token.label: Connect REST API session token
rest_session_token.setup: Obtain a session token from the Agent Browser UI Extensibility Framework `IExtensionGlobalContext.getSessionToken`, or generate it with a custom PHP script that calls `AgentAuthenticator::authenticateCredentials(...)` and returns `session_id`, as shown in [Session authentication for REST API](https://cx.rightnow.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/11153). This token is then sent as `Authorization: Session <token>`.

em_jwt.label: Element Manager JWT bearer token
em_jwt.setup: Configure an external identity provider for the site under Single Sign-On Configurations > OAUTH, upload the public certificate, and create or use an agent account with `SSO Login (SAML 2.0)` enabled. Then generate an `RS256` JWT matching the documented claims (`sub`, `iss`, `exp`, `jti`, `aud`) as described in [Element Manager Public API authentication](https://cx.rightnow.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/11065).

em_public_api_token.label: Element Manager public API token
em_public_api_token.setup: First generate an Element Manager public API token by calling `POST /api/elementmanager/authentication/authToken` with a JWT bearer token and `interfaceUrl` header, as described in [Element Manager Public API authentication](https://cx.rightnow.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/11065). Use the returned token to call Element Manager public REST endpoints.

chat_wsse.label: Chat API WS-Security username/password plus AppID
chat_wsse.setup: Use an Oracle B2C Service user account that can access Chat APIs, then send WS-Security `UsernameToken` credentials in the SOAP header together with a client `AppID` in the `ClientRequestHeader`, as shown in [Simple example of how to authenticate to the Chat UQ API](https://cx.rightnow.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/8601).

surfaces.name: Chat SOAP API / Universal Queue API
